question: A project manager is initiating a new software development project. The customer has high expectations for product reliability. During planning, the sponsor suggests skipping formal quality planning to save time and address quality issues as they arise. What should the PM do?
options: Develop a comprehensive quality management plan that defines standards, metrics, and acceptance criteria before development begins
Agree with the sponsor and address quality defects reactively through inspection at project completion
Defer quality planning until the first major deliverable is ready for review
Assign quality control activities to the testing team and proceed with development
explanation: PMI emphasizes planning quality in from the start. A quality management plan establishes standards, metrics, and acceptance criteria early, enabling prevention rather than reactive inspection. Addressing quality reactively increases the cost of non-conformance significantly.
explanation_vi: PMI nhấn mạnh việc lập kế hoạch chất lượng ngay từ đầu. Kế hoạch quản lý chất lượng thiết lập tiêu chuẩn, chỉ số và tiêu chí chấp nhận từ sớm, cho phép phòng ngừa thay vì kiểm tra phản ứng. Giải quyết chất lượng theo hướng phản ứng làm tăng đáng kể chi phí không tuân thủ.

question: During project execution, the PM discovers that 80% of product defects are caused by only 3 of the 15 identified risk areas. The team wants to spread quality improvement efforts equally across all 15 areas. What should the PM recommend?
options: Focus quality improvement efforts on the 3 areas causing 80% of defects, using a Pareto chart to prioritize
Distribute improvement resources equally across all 15 areas to ensure comprehensive coverage
Escalate to the sponsor to request additional resources to address all 15 areas simultaneously
Implement a fishbone diagram to explore all 15 areas before deciding on priorities
explanation: The Pareto principle (80/20 rule) states that roughly 80% of effects come from 20% of causes. A Pareto chart visually identifies the vital few causes. The PM should focus resources on the 3 areas driving most defects for maximum quality improvement impact.
explanation_vi: Nguyên tắc Pareto (quy tắc 80/20) cho rằng khoảng 80% kết quả đến từ 20% nguyên nhân. Biểu đồ Pareto xác định trực quan các nguyên nhân quan trọng. PM nên tập trung nguồn lực vào 3 lĩnh vực gây ra nhiều lỗi nhất để tối đa hóa tác động cải thiện chất lượng.

question: A quality audit reveals that the project team is consistently skipping required code reviews, bypassing the defined process to meet schedule deadlines. The PM is under pressure from the sponsor to deliver on time. What should the PM do first?
options: Conduct a quality audit finding meeting, document the non-compliance, and implement corrective actions to restore the review process
Allow the team to skip reviews temporarily to meet the deadline and reintroduce reviews in the next phase
Reduce the scope of code reviews to a lighter checklist that can be completed faster
Escalate immediately to the sponsor and request a schedule extension before taking any other action
explanation: Quality audits are a QA activity designed to confirm adherence to processes. When non-compliance is found, the PM must document findings and implement corrective actions. Allowing process bypasses sets a dangerous precedent and increases the cost of defects that reach customers.
explanation_vi: Kiểm toán chất lượng là hoạt động QA được thiết kế để xác nhận việc tuân thủ quy trình. Khi phát hiện không tuân thủ, PM phải ghi lại phát hiện và thực hiện hành động khắc phục. Cho phép bỏ qua quy trình tạo tiền lệ nguy hiểm và làm tăng chi phí lỗi đến tay khách hàng.

question: An agile team is preparing for their first sprint. The product owner wants to defer writing automated tests until after the core features are built to avoid slowing down initial development. What should the Scrum Master advise?
options: Implement Test-Driven Development (TDD) so that automated tests are written before the code, ensuring quality is built in from the start
Agree with the product owner and schedule a dedicated testing sprint after all features are complete
Write tests in parallel with development but as a separate workstream to avoid blocking developers
Use manual testing for initial sprints and introduce automation only after the codebase stabilizes
explanation: TDD is an agile quality practice where tests are written before code, ensuring every feature has test coverage from the start. This prevents defect accumulation and avoids the costly 'testing sprint' anti-pattern, which delays feedback and increases rework.
explanation_vi: TDD là thực hành chất lượng agile trong đó kiểm thử được viết trước code, đảm bảo mọi tính năng đều có kiểm thử từ đầu. Điều này ngăn ngừa tích lũy lỗi và tránh anti-pattern 'sprint kiểm thử', làm trễ phản hồi và tăng công việc làm lại.

question: A product deliverable passes all technical specifications but receives negative customer feedback because it is difficult to use. The PM argues the product conforms to requirements, so quality is acceptable. What concept is the PM overlooking?
options: Fitness for use — quality must also mean the product effectively serves the customer's intended purpose, not just meet documented specs
Grade — the product may be low grade even if high quality, which is acceptable if the customer agreed to the grade
Cost of quality — the PM should calculate whether customer satisfaction activities justify the additional cost
Gold plating — adding usability improvements beyond requirements would be scope creep and should be avoided
explanation: Quality has two dimensions: conformance to requirements AND fitness for use. A product that meets specs but fails to satisfy the customer has not achieved true quality. Customer satisfaction is the ultimate measure of quality in PMI's framework.
explanation_vi: Chất lượng có hai chiều: tuân thủ yêu cầu VÀ phù hợp với mục đích sử dụng. Sản phẩm đáp ứng thông số kỹ thuật nhưng không làm hài lòng khách hàng chưa đạt được chất lượng thực sự. Sự hài lòng của khách hàng là thước đo chất lượng cuối cùng trong khung PMI.

question: The project team is repeatedly encountering the same type of defect in manufactured components. The PM wants to understand the root causes before deciding on corrective actions. Which quality tool should be used first?
options: Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram to categorize and analyze potential root causes across multiple dimensions
Control chart to determine if the defect rate is within acceptable statistical limits
Pareto chart to identify which defect type contributes most to overall defect count
Checklist to verify that all manufacturing steps were completed correctly
explanation: The Ishikawa (fishbone or cause-and-effect) diagram is the right tool for root cause analysis. It organizes potential causes into categories (people, process, equipment, materials, environment, measurement) to systematically explore why a defect occurs before selecting corrective actions.
explanation_vi: Biểu đồ Ishikawa (xương cá hay nhân quả) là công cụ phù hợp để phân tích nguyên nhân gốc rễ. Nó tổ chức các nguyên nhân tiềm năng thành các danh mục (con người, quy trình, thiết bị, vật liệu, môi trường, đo lường) để khám phá có hệ thống lý do xảy ra lỗi trước khi chọn hành động khắc phục.

question: Management is pressuring the PM to cut the project budget by 15%. The sponsor proposes eliminating testing and inspection activities to achieve the savings. What should the PM explain about the cost of quality?
options: Eliminating testing reduces appraisal costs but will likely increase external failure costs (warranty, rework, customer complaints) far exceeding the savings
The savings are justified because the team can rely on developers to self-check their work instead of formal testing
Cutting testing is acceptable if the project inserts a customer acceptance phase at the end to catch major issues
The cost of quality is a sunk cost; savings from cutting testing now outweigh future defect costs
explanation: Cost of Quality includes prevention costs, appraisal costs, and failure costs (internal and external). External failure costs — defects found by customers — are the most expensive category. Cutting appraisal (testing) increases the likelihood of external failures, which typically cost far more than the testing savings.
explanation_vi: Chi phí chất lượng bao gồm chi phí phòng ngừa, chi phí thẩm định và chi phí thất bại (nội bộ và bên ngoài). Chi phí thất bại bên ngoài — lỗi do khách hàng phát hiện — là danh mục đắt nhất. Cắt giảm thẩm định (kiểm thử) làm tăng khả năng thất bại bên ngoài, thường tốn kém hơn nhiều so với khoản tiết kiệm từ kiểm thử.

question: During a sprint retrospective, the team identifies that integration issues discovered late in each sprint are causing significant rework. The team wants to address this systemic problem. What is the best approach aligned with agile quality principles?
options: Implement continuous integration (CI) so code is integrated and tested automatically with every commit, catching integration issues immediately
Add a dedicated integration testing week at the end of each sprint to thoroughly verify integrations before review
Assign a senior developer to manually review all integrations at the end of each sprint
Log the integration issues as technical debt items and address them in a dedicated hardening sprint
explanation: Continuous integration is a core agile engineering practice that automatically builds and tests code on every commit. This surfaces integration problems immediately when they are cheapest to fix, embodying the agile principle of building quality in rather than inspecting it at the end.
explanation_vi: Tích hợp liên tục là thực hành kỹ thuật agile cốt lõi tự động build và kiểm thử code trên mỗi commit. Điều này phát hiện vấn đề tích hợp ngay lập tức khi chúng rẻ nhất để sửa, thể hiện nguyên tắc agile xây dựng chất lượng từ bên trong thay vì kiểm tra ở cuối.

question: A control chart for a manufacturing process shows data points consistently trending toward the upper control limit over the past eight measurements, though no point has exceeded the limit yet. What should the PM do?
options: Investigate and address the trend proactively, as a consistent run of points trending in one direction indicates an out-of-control process
Take no action since all data points are still within the control limits and the process is statistically in control
Widen the control limits to accommodate the trend and continue monitoring
Wait until a data point exceeds the control limit before initiating a process investigation
explanation: In control chart theory, a run of seven or more consecutive points trending in one direction (even within limits) signals a non-random pattern indicating the process is going out of control. PMI expects PMs to act proactively on these signals rather than waiting for a breach.
explanation_vi: Trong lý thuyết biểu đồ kiểm soát, một chuỗi bảy điểm liên tiếp theo xu hướng một chiều (dù trong giới hạn) báo hiệu mẫu không ngẫu nhiên cho thấy quy trình đang mất kiểm soát. PMI kỳ vọng PM hành động chủ động với các tín hiệu này thay vì chờ vượt ngưỡng.

question: A hybrid project delivers regulatory compliance reports using predictive methods while developing a digital dashboard using agile sprints. The compliance team insists all work follow the same formal quality checklist. What should the PM do?
options: Apply the formal compliance checklist to regulatory deliverables and use agile quality practices (TDD, automated testing, retrospectives) for the digital dashboard
Standardize all work under the formal compliance checklist to ensure consistency across both workstreams
Apply agile quality practices to both workstreams since they share the same development team
Defer quality planning for the digital dashboard until the regulatory deliverables are complete
explanation: In hybrid projects, quality approaches should be tailored to the nature of each workstream. Regulatory deliverables require formal checklists and compliance verification, while agile components benefit from iterative quality practices. A one-size-fits-all approach can be inefficient or insufficient.
explanation_vi: Trong dự án hybrid, phương pháp chất lượng nên được điều chỉnh theo tính chất của từng luồng công việc. Các sản phẩm quy định cần danh sách kiểm tra chính thức và xác minh tuân thủ, trong khi các thành phần agile hưởng lợi từ thực hành chất lượng lặp đi lặp lại. Phương pháp một kích thước phù hợp tất cả có thể không hiệu quả hoặc không đủ.

question: The PM is developing a quality management plan and must define acceptance criteria for deliverables. A team member argues that acceptance criteria should be defined by the project team to maintain technical accuracy. What should the PM do?
options: Involve both the customer and the project team in defining acceptance criteria to ensure they are technically accurate and aligned with customer needs
Allow the project team to define acceptance criteria based on their technical expertise, then present them to the customer for sign-off
Have the customer define all acceptance criteria independently to ensure the project fully satisfies their requirements
Use industry-standard benchmarks as acceptance criteria to avoid disputes between the team and the customer
explanation: Acceptance criteria must reflect both technical feasibility and customer requirements. Involving both parties ensures criteria are measurable, technically sound, and aligned with the customer's definition of done. This prevents misalignment that leads to rework or disputes at project closure.
explanation_vi: Tiêu chí chấp nhận phải phản ánh cả tính khả thi kỹ thuật và yêu cầu của khách hàng. Sự tham gia của cả hai bên đảm bảo tiêu chí có thể đo lường, có cơ sở kỹ thuật và phù hợp với định nghĩa hoàn thành của khách hàng. Điều này ngăn ngừa sự không đồng thuận dẫn đến làm lại hoặc tranh chấp khi kết thúc dự án.

question: An agile team has been delivering features each sprint, but stakeholders report that new features frequently break previously working functionality. The team spends significant time in each sprint diagnosing regressions. What should the PM recommend?
options: Build an automated regression test suite that runs with every build to detect broken functionality immediately before it accumulates
Dedicate one team member per sprint to manually re-test all previously delivered features after new code is added
Implement a formal change control process requiring stakeholder approval before any new code is merged
Reduce the number of features per sprint to give the team more time to manually verify existing functionality
explanation: Automated regression testing is the agile quality practice that catches unintended breakage of existing features when new code is added. It enables the team to maintain a potentially releasable product increment every sprint without the manual overhead of re-testing everything.
explanation_vi: Kiểm thử hồi quy tự động là thực hành chất lượng agile phát hiện sự cố không mong muốn của các tính năng hiện có khi thêm code mới. Nó cho phép nhóm duy trì phần tăng thêm sản phẩm có thể phát hành mỗi sprint mà không cần tốn công sức kiểm tra lại mọi thứ thủ công.

question: A project manager is distinguishing between quality assurance and quality control activities. The team has been conducting code walkthroughs, process audits, and reviewing the testing approach. Which category do these activities fall under?
options: Quality Assurance (QA) — these are process-oriented activities focused on preventing defects by ensuring the right processes are followed
Quality Control (QC) — these are inspection activities that examine outputs to identify defects
Quality Planning — these are activities to establish the quality standards and procedures for the project
Continuous improvement — these activities are part of the Kaizen process to incrementally improve project processes
explanation: QA activities are process-oriented and preventive: audits, walkthroughs, and process reviews ensure the team is following correct procedures to prevent defects from occurring. QC is inspection-oriented: examining actual outputs (test results, deliverables) to find defects after they exist.
explanation_vi: Các hoạt động QA hướng quy trình và phòng ngừa: kiểm toán, xem xét kỹ lưỡng và đánh giá quy trình đảm bảo nhóm tuân theo quy trình đúng để ngăn lỗi xảy ra. QC hướng kiểm tra: xem xét các đầu ra thực tế (kết quả kiểm thử, sản phẩm) để tìm lỗi sau khi chúng đã tồn tại.

question: A manufacturing project has high internal failure costs (scrap, rework) but low external failure costs. Management wants to reduce total cost by eliminating prevention activities like process training and design reviews. What should the PM advise?
options: Prevention costs are the least expensive category in the cost of quality model; investing in prevention reduces both internal and external failure costs
Agree that prevention activities can be reduced since external failure costs are already low
Replace prevention activities with additional inspection to find defects before they cause internal failures
Reduce prevention activities gradually while monitoring whether internal failure costs increase
explanation: In the Cost of Quality model, prevention costs (training, process design, design reviews) are the cheapest investment. Prevention reduces defects at their source, which reduces both internal failure costs (rework, scrap) and external failure costs (warranty, customer complaints). Cutting prevention typically increases total quality costs.
explanation_vi: Trong mô hình Chi phí Chất lượng, chi phí phòng ngừa (đào tạo, thiết kế quy trình, đánh giá thiết kế) là khoản đầu tư rẻ nhất. Phòng ngừa giảm lỗi tại nguồn, từ đó giảm cả chi phí thất bại nội bộ (làm lại, phế liệu) và chi phí thất bại bên ngoài (bảo hành, khiếu nại khách hàng). Cắt giảm phòng ngừa thường làm tăng tổng chi phí chất lượng.

question: A product owner wants to add story acceptance criteria only after a feature is developed to avoid constraining the developers' creativity. The development team supports this approach. What should the agile PM clarify?
options: Acceptance criteria must be defined before development begins; they are part of the Definition of Ready and enable the team to build the right solution from the start
The product owner's approach is valid in agile since requirements emerge over time and late specification reduces waste
Acceptance criteria can be added post-development as long as stakeholders review them before the sprint review
Developers should define their own acceptance criteria to maintain technical accuracy and creative freedom
explanation: In agile, acceptance criteria are defined as part of story refinement before a story enters a sprint (Definition of Ready). Pre-defined criteria guide developers on what 'done' means, enabling them to write correct tests (TDD/BDD) and build the right solution. Post-hoc criteria lead to rework and disputes.
explanation_vi: Trong agile, tiêu chí chấp nhận được xác định như một phần của việc tinh chỉnh story trước khi story vào sprint (Định nghĩa Sẵn sàng). Tiêu chí được xác định trước hướng dẫn nhà phát triển về ý nghĩa của 'hoàn thành', cho phép họ viết kiểm thử đúng (TDD/BDD) và xây dựng giải pháp phù hợp. Tiêu chí sau thực tế dẫn đến làm lại và tranh chấp.

question: A hybrid project team completes a sprint and the PM conducts a retrospective. The team identifies that unclear handoff documentation between the agile sprint team and the predictive delivery team is causing integration defects. What is the best quality improvement action?
options: Use retrospective findings to create standardized handoff templates and acceptance criteria between the two workstreams as a continuous improvement action
Assign a dedicated liaison between the two teams to manually verify all handoffs each sprint
Revert the agile workstream to predictive methods to standardize documentation practices across the project
Log the issue in the risk register and schedule a lessons-learned review at project close
explanation: Retrospectives are a quality improvement mechanism in agile and hybrid projects. When a systemic process issue (unclear handoffs) is identified, the PM should use the retrospective output to implement a process improvement — standardized templates — addressing root causes rather than adding manual workarounds.
explanation_vi: Retrospectives là cơ chế cải tiến chất lượng trong các dự án agile và hybrid. Khi một vấn đề quy trình có hệ thống (bàn giao không rõ ràng) được xác định, PM nên sử dụng kết quả retrospective để thực hiện cải tiến quy trình — các mẫu chuẩn hóa — giải quyết nguyên nhân gốc rễ thay vì thêm cách giải quyết thủ công.

question: A project team is using Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce defects in a production process. After completing the Define and Measure phases, the team has baseline data showing a defect rate of 4.2 sigma. What is the next correct step?
options: Analyze phase — use root cause analysis tools to identify the key factors causing the current defect rate before attempting improvements
Improve phase — immediately implement process changes based on the measurement data to begin reducing defects
Control phase — establish control limits and monitoring to maintain the current performance level
Define phase again — revise the problem statement to target a higher sigma level than 4.2
explanation: DMAIC is sequential: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control. After measuring baseline performance, the Analyze phase identifies root causes of the gap between current and desired performance. Jumping to Improve without analysis risks implementing solutions for the wrong causes.
explanation_vi: DMAIC có thứ tự: Xác định, Đo lường, Phân tích, Cải tiến, Kiểm soát. Sau khi đo lường hiệu suất cơ sở, giai đoạn Phân tích xác định nguyên nhân gốc rễ của khoảng cách giữa hiệu suất hiện tại và mong muốn. Bỏ qua sang Cải tiến mà không phân tích có nguy cơ thực hiện giải pháp cho sai nguyên nhân.

question: A project manager is developing a quality management plan. Which of the following are prevention costs in the Cost of Quality framework? (Select 2)
options: Employee training on quality standards and procedures
Product testing and inspection before customer delivery
Reworking a defective component discovered during manufacturing
Designing quality requirements into the product design process
explanation: Prevention costs are investments made to prevent defects from occurring: training (option A) and design quality activities (option D). Testing/inspection is an appraisal cost (option B). Rework is an internal failure cost (option C). Prevention is the least expensive CoQ category.
explanation_vi: Chi phí phòng ngừa là các khoản đầu tư để ngăn lỗi xảy ra: đào tạo (phương án A) và hoạt động thiết kế chất lượng (phương án D). Kiểm thử/kiểm tra là chi phí thẩm định (phương án B). Làm lại là chi phí thất bại nội bộ (phương án C). Phòng ngừa là danh mục CoQ rẻ nhất.

question: A Scrum team delivers a working increment each sprint, but the product owner notices that features meet the acceptance criteria yet still feel incomplete or suboptimal to end users. The team argues that they delivered per spec. What agile quality concept is being missed?
options: Customer satisfaction is the ultimate quality measure — the team should involve end users in sprint reviews to validate that the product truly meets their needs beyond written criteria
The team is gold-plating by adding features beyond what's specified; the product owner should tighten acceptance criteria
The sprint review is a formal quality control gate; the product owner should reject the increment and request rework
The definition of done should be expanded to include additional technical checks to improve output quality
explanation: In agile, quality is ultimately defined by customer satisfaction and value delivered, not just compliance with written criteria. Sprint reviews should include end-user feedback to validate fitness for use. Written acceptance criteria capture known requirements but cannot fully substitute for real user validation.
explanation_vi: Trong agile, chất lượng cuối cùng được xác định bởi sự hài lòng của khách hàng và giá trị mang lại, không chỉ là tuân thủ tiêu chí viết sẵn. Sprint reviews nên bao gồm phản hồi người dùng cuối để xác nhận phù hợp với mục đích sử dụng. Tiêu chí chấp nhận bằng văn bản nắm bắt các yêu cầu đã biết nhưng không thể thay thế hoàn toàn cho xác nhận từ người dùng thực.

question: During quality planning, the PM defines quality metrics for a software project: defect density (defects per KLOC), test coverage percentage, and mean time to failure. A stakeholder asks why these metrics are needed when the team already has a testing phase. What should the PM explain?
options: Quality metrics provide objective, measurable evidence of product quality throughout development, enabling early detection of quality trends before the testing phase
Metrics are required by PMI standards but add no practical value beyond what the testing phase already provides
Metrics are used primarily in project reporting to demonstrate compliance to senior management
The testing phase is sufficient; additional metrics create unnecessary administrative overhead for the team
explanation: Quality metrics defined during planning establish objective baselines for measuring quality throughout the project. They enable trend analysis and early warning signals, supporting the PMI principle of preventing defects rather than discovering them only at the end through a testing phase.
explanation_vi: Các chỉ số chất lượng được xác định trong quá trình lập kế hoạch thiết lập cơ sở đo lường khách quan cho chất lượng trong suốt dự án. Chúng cho phép phân tích xu hướng và tín hiệu cảnh báo sớm, hỗ trợ nguyên tắc PMI phòng ngừa lỗi thay vì chỉ phát hiện chúng ở cuối thông qua giai đoạn kiểm thử.

question: A hybrid project manager notices that the agile team's Definition of Done (DoD) does not align with the project's overall quality standards defined in the quality management plan. Sprint increments are being accepted that would fail the project's formal quality checklist. What should the PM do?
options: Update the team's Definition of Done to incorporate the relevant project quality standards, ensuring sprint increments meet both agile completeness criteria and project quality requirements
Create a separate quality review gate after each sprint that applies the formal quality checklist independently of the DoD
Allow the agile team to maintain their own DoD since quality standards apply only to formal deliverables, not sprint increments
Replace the agile DoD with the formal quality checklist to standardize quality verification across the project
explanation: The Definition of Done is the agile team's quality standard for sprint work. In a hybrid project, the DoD must incorporate project-level quality requirements so increments meet both agile and organizational quality standards. Separate gates after the DoD indicate a misaligned quality process.
explanation_vi: Định nghĩa Hoàn thành là tiêu chuẩn chất lượng của nhóm agile cho công việc sprint. Trong dự án hybrid, DoD phải tích hợp các yêu cầu chất lượng cấp dự án để các phần tăng thêm đáp ứng cả tiêu chuẩn chất lượng agile và tổ chức. Các cổng kiểm tra riêng biệt sau DoD cho thấy quy trình chất lượng không được căn chỉnh.

question: A delivered project component fails in the field, causing customer downtime and warranty repairs costing $500,000. A post-mortem reveals the defect was detectable with a $15,000 inspection during manufacturing. Which cost of quality category does the $500,000 represent, and what does this illustrate?
options: External failure cost — the most expensive CoQ category, illustrating that investing in prevention and appraisal is far cheaper than allowing defects to reach customers
Internal failure cost — the component failed during manufacturing, not in the field, so prevention was the right investment
Appraisal cost — the $500,000 represents the cost of the inspection that was skipped, not a failure cost
Prevention cost — the money spent on redesign to prevent future occurrences of the same defect
explanation: External failure costs are defects found by customers after delivery: warranty, repairs, liability, reputation damage. These are the most expensive CoQ category. The scenario illustrates that skipping $15,000 in appraisal costs led to $500,000 in external failure costs — a 33x cost multiplier, demonstrating why prevention and appraisal investments are economically rational.
explanation_vi: Chi phí thất bại bên ngoài là các lỗi do khách hàng phát hiện sau khi giao hàng: bảo hành, sửa chữa, trách nhiệm pháp lý, thiệt hại danh tiếng. Đây là danh mục CoQ đắt nhất. Tình huống minh họa rằng bỏ qua $15.000 chi phí thẩm định dẫn đến $500.000 chi phí thất bại bên ngoài — hệ số chi phí 33 lần, chứng minh tại sao đầu tư vào phòng ngừa và thẩm định là hợp lý về mặt kinh tế.

question: An agile team is practicing Behavior-Driven Development (BDD). A new developer asks why scenarios are written in plain language (Given-When-Then) rather than technical test code. What is the primary quality benefit of BDD?
options: BDD scenarios serve as both requirements and tests, enabling shared understanding between business stakeholders and developers, reducing misinterpretation defects
BDD is faster to execute than unit tests because plain language scenarios require less processing overhead
BDD replaces the need for acceptance criteria since scenarios fully define what needs to be built
BDD allows developers to skip unit testing since behavior tests provide sufficient coverage
explanation: BDD bridges the communication gap between business and technical teams by expressing requirements as executable scenarios (Given-When-Then). This shared language reduces requirements misinterpretation — one of the most costly defect sources — and ensures tests validate business behavior, not just technical implementation.
explanation_vi: BDD thu hẹp khoảng cách giao tiếp giữa nhóm kinh doanh và kỹ thuật bằng cách diễn đạt yêu cầu dưới dạng kịch bản có thể thực thi (Given-When-Then). Ngôn ngữ chung này giảm sự hiểu sai yêu cầu — một trong những nguồn lỗi tốn kém nhất — và đảm bảo kiểm thử xác nhận hành vi kinh doanh, không chỉ thực hiện kỹ thuật.

question: A large infrastructure project is nearing completion. The sponsor proposes skipping the planned quality audits for the last three work packages to compress the schedule by two weeks. What should the PM do?
options: Decline the request and explain that skipping audits removes process verification, increasing the risk of undiscovered defects that could be far more costly to fix post-delivery
Agree to skip audits but increase final inspection activities to compensate
Skip audits for the two lower-risk work packages but keep the audit for the highest-risk one
Accept the schedule compression and document it as an approved risk in the risk register
explanation: Quality audits verify that quality processes are being followed — a QA activity that prevents defects from accumulating undetected. Skipping audits increases conformance risk and non-conformance costs. The PM should push back, citing that short-term schedule gains typically lead to longer-term rework costs.
explanation_vi: Kiểm toán chất lượng xác minh rằng các quy trình chất lượng đang được tuân thủ — hoạt động QA ngăn ngừa lỗi tích lũy mà không bị phát hiện. Bỏ qua kiểm toán làm tăng rủi ro tuân thủ và chi phí không tuân thủ. PM nên phản đối, viện dẫn rằng lợi ích tiến độ ngắn hạn thường dẫn đến chi phí làm lại dài hạn.

question: After a product launch, the PM receives a report showing a high rate of customer complaints about product reliability. The PM wants to systematically improve quality in future projects. Which approach best aligns with continuous improvement principles?
options: Conduct a root cause analysis, document lessons learned, update process assets, and apply improvements to the organization's quality processes and future project planning
Implement more rigorous final testing before product launch to catch defects before they reach customers
Add a customer satisfaction survey post-launch to better understand and track quality perceptions
Create a dedicated quality improvement project to redesign the manufacturing process
explanation: Continuous improvement (Kaizen) requires systematically learning from quality failures and embedding improvements into organizational processes. Root cause analysis, lessons learned, and process asset updates ensure improvements are institutionalized and applied to future projects, not just addressed reactively in the current one.
explanation_vi: Cải tiến liên tục (Kaizen) đòi hỏi học hỏi có hệ thống từ các thất bại chất lượng và tích hợp cải tiến vào quy trình tổ chức. Phân tích nguyên nhân gốc rễ, bài học kinh nghiệm và cập nhật tài sản quy trình đảm bảo cải tiến được thể chế hóa và áp dụng cho các dự án tương lai, không chỉ giải quyết phản ứng trong dự án hiện tại.

question: A PM is performing quality control on a batch of manufactured parts. Measurements show that 95% of parts are within specification. The PM declares quality is acceptable. A quality engineer disagrees, pointing out that several measurements are clustered near the upper specification limit. Who is correct and why?
options: The quality engineer is correct — clustering near specification limits indicates process drift that a control chart would reveal, and proactive adjustment is needed before parts begin failing
The PM is correct — 95% conformance exceeds most industry standards and no further action is required
Both are correct — the PM should document the clustering as an observation while proceeding with delivery
The quality engineer is correct, but only if the upper specification limit is a critical safety parameter
explanation: Statistical process control looks beyond pass/fail rates to process behavior. Clustering near a limit indicates the process is drifting toward non-conformance. A control chart would show this trend. Proactive correction (before parts fail) is cheaper than waiting for failures — consistent with the prevention-over-inspection mindset.
explanation_vi: Kiểm soát quy trình thống kê nhìn xa hơn tỷ lệ đạt/không đạt để xem hành vi quy trình. Sự tụ tập gần giới hạn cho thấy quy trình đang trôi dạt về phía không tuân thủ. Biểu đồ kiểm soát sẽ hiển thị xu hướng này. Hiệu chỉnh chủ động (trước khi bộ phận thất bại) rẻ hơn chờ thất bại — nhất quán với tư duy phòng ngừa hơn kiểm tra.

question: An agile team's sprint velocity has been declining over three sprints. The Scrum Master notices that increasing technical debt is forcing developers to spend more time on workarounds than new features. How does this relate to quality management?
options: Technical debt represents deferred quality costs; addressing it through refactoring is a quality investment that improves long-term productivity and reduces internal failure costs
Technical debt is a normal part of agile development and should be tracked but not prioritized over feature delivery
Velocity decline is a capacity issue, not a quality issue; the PM should add resources to restore throughput
The team should adopt stricter code review processes to prevent technical debt from accumulating in future sprints
explanation: Technical debt is an internal quality cost — deferred quality work that accumulates interest in the form of increased complexity, slower development, and higher defect rates. In agile quality management, refactoring technical debt is equivalent to addressing internal failure costs, and is a legitimate quality investment that improves sustainable velocity.
explanation_vi: Nợ kỹ thuật là chi phí chất lượng nội bộ — công việc chất lượng bị hoãn lại tích lũy lãi dưới dạng tăng phức tạp, phát triển chậm hơn và tỷ lệ lỗi cao hơn. Trong quản lý chất lượng agile, tái cấu trúc nợ kỹ thuật tương đương với việc giải quyết chi phí thất bại nội bộ và là khoản đầu tư chất lượng hợp lệ cải thiện vận tốc bền vững.

question: A hybrid project PM is tailoring the quality management approach. Which of the following practices should be included to support quality in both agile and predictive workstreams? (Select 2)
options: Retrospectives to identify and implement process improvements continuously
A single gate inspection at project completion to verify all deliverables meet acceptance criteria
Quality metrics defined in the quality management plan applicable to all workstreams
Deferring quality verification to the final integration phase to avoid disrupting sprint velocity
explanation: Retrospectives (option A) support continuous improvement across both workstreams. Project-level quality metrics (option C) provide consistent standards applicable to both predictive and agile work. Single end-gate inspection (option B) contradicts prevention principles. Deferring quality verification (option D) increases late-discovery costs.
explanation_vi: Retrospectives (phương án A) hỗ trợ cải tiến liên tục trên cả hai luồng công việc. Chỉ số chất lượng cấp dự án (phương án C) cung cấp tiêu chuẩn nhất quán áp dụng cho cả công việc dự đoán và agile. Kiểm tra cổng đơn cuối cùng (phương án B) mâu thuẫn với nguyên tắc phòng ngừa. Hoãn xác minh chất lượng (phương án D) làm tăng chi phí phát hiện muộn.

question: A project quality checklist was developed during planning and has been used without update for six months. A team member suggests the checklist may be outdated as the project scope evolved. What should the PM do?
options: Review and update the quality checklist through integrated change control to reflect current scope and quality requirements
Continue using the original checklist since it was approved during planning and changing it would require re-baselining quality
Create a supplemental checklist to cover new scope items while keeping the original for existing scope
Ask team members to use their judgment to fill gaps in the checklist where scope has changed
explanation: Quality documents, including checklists, must be kept current with approved scope changes. An outdated checklist creates quality gaps where new scope items are not verified. The PM should process updates through integrated change control to maintain a valid, comprehensive quality verification baseline.
explanation_vi: Các tài liệu chất lượng, bao gồm danh sách kiểm tra, phải được cập nhật với các thay đổi phạm vi được phê duyệt. Danh sách kiểm tra lỗi thời tạo ra khoảng trống chất lượng nơi các mục phạm vi mới không được xác minh. PM nên xử lý các cập nhật thông qua kiểm soát thay đổi tích hợp để duy trì cơ sở xác minh chất lượng hợp lệ, toàn diện.

question: A Scrum team is under pressure to increase sprint velocity. A developer proposes bypassing the peer code review step in the Definition of Done since it adds 2-4 hours per story. The product owner agrees. What should the Scrum Master advise?
options: Code review is a prevention activity; removing it will reduce short-term cycle time but increase defect rates, technical debt, and long-term rework costs, so it should be retained
Agree to remove code reviews temporarily to improve velocity, with a plan to reintroduce them when the team has more capacity
Replace code review with automated static analysis tools to achieve the same quality outcome faster
Reduce the scope of code reviews to a quick 15-minute check to balance quality and velocity
explanation: Code reviews in the DoD are a prevention activity. Their removal trades short-term velocity gains for increased defect injection, technical debt accumulation, and long-term rework — a classic false economy. The Scrum Master should educate the team on this quality cost trade-off rather than accommodating the shortcut.
explanation_vi: Đánh giá code trong DoD là hoạt động phòng ngừa. Việc loại bỏ chúng đánh đổi lợi ích vận tốc ngắn hạn với việc tăng lỗi, tích lũy nợ kỹ thuật và làm lại dài hạn — một sự kinh tế giả cổ điển. Scrum Master nên giáo dục nhóm về sự đánh đổi chi phí chất lượng này thay vì chấp nhận lối tắt.

question: A project manager needs to improve a repetitive manufacturing process that has had elevated defect rates for two months. Which continuous improvement methodology uses five sequential phases to structure the improvement effort?
options: Six Sigma DMAIC — Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control — provides a data-driven framework for eliminating defects systematically
Lean manufacturing — focuses on eliminating waste (muda) and improving flow to increase efficiency and reduce defects
Kaizen — small incremental improvements made continuously by all employees to gradually reduce defects
PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) — a four-phase iterative cycle for continuous process improvement
explanation: DMAIC (Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control) is the Six Sigma methodology specifically structured in five sequential phases for systematic defect reduction. PDCA has four phases, Lean focuses on waste elimination, and Kaizen is an approach/philosophy rather than a structured 5-phase methodology.
explanation_vi: DMAIC (Xác định, Đo lường, Phân tích, Cải tiến, Kiểm soát) là phương pháp Six Sigma được cấu trúc cụ thể trong năm giai đoạn tuần tự để giảm lỗi có hệ thống. PDCA có bốn giai đoạn, Lean tập trung vào loại bỏ lãng phí, và Kaizen là phương pháp/triết lý hơn là phương pháp luận 5 giai đoạn có cấu trúc.

question: A hybrid project delivers a regulated medical device (predictive) with supporting mobile app features (agile). The regulatory deliverables require full traceability from requirements to test results. Agile features do not have this requirement. How should the PM structure quality management?
options: Apply full requirements traceability and formal test documentation to regulatory deliverables, while using story-based acceptance criteria and automated tests for agile features
Apply full traceability requirements to all deliverables to ensure consistency and avoid confusion in the quality records
Apply agile practices to all deliverables to reduce documentation overhead and improve team efficiency
Separate the regulatory and agile workstreams into independent projects with separate PMs to avoid quality process conflicts
explanation: Hybrid quality management requires tailoring quality practices to the regulatory and compliance requirements of each workstream. Applying unnecessary rigor to agile features wastes resources; applying insufficient rigor to regulated deliverables creates compliance risk. The PM should tailor quality approaches proportionally.
explanation_vi: Quản lý chất lượng hybrid đòi hỏi điều chỉnh thực hành chất lượng theo yêu cầu quy định và tuân thủ của từng luồng công việc. Áp dụng tiêu chuẩn không cần thiết cho các tính năng agile lãng phí tài nguyên; áp dụng tiêu chuẩn không đủ cho các sản phẩm quy định tạo ra rủi ro tuân thủ. PM nên điều chỉnh phương pháp chất lượng theo tỷ lệ.

question: A quality manager informs the PM that the project's cost of non-conformance is four times the cost of conformance. The PM is under budget pressure. What does this indicate and what should the PM recommend?
options: The project is under-investing in quality activities; increasing prevention and appraisal spending will reduce total project costs by dramatically cutting non-conformance costs
The quality budget is already over-allocated; the PM should reduce quality activities to bring costs into balance
The cost of non-conformance is acceptable since it is less than five times the conformance cost per industry standards
Both cost categories should be reduced proportionally to achieve overall budget savings
explanation: When non-conformance costs (rework, defects, failures) far exceed conformance costs (prevention + appraisal), it signals under-investment in quality. Increasing prevention/appraisal investments typically reduces non-conformance costs by a greater amount, lowering total quality costs. This is a core PMI cost of quality principle.
explanation_vi: Khi chi phí không tuân thủ (làm lại, lỗi, thất bại) vượt xa chi phí tuân thủ (phòng ngừa + thẩm định), điều đó báo hiệu đầu tư không đủ vào chất lượng. Tăng đầu tư phòng ngừa/thẩm định thường giảm chi phí không tuân thủ nhiều hơn, từ đó giảm tổng chi phí chất lượng. Đây là nguyên tắc chi phí chất lượng cốt lõi của PMI.

question: During a sprint retrospective, an agile team identifies three recurring process issues: slow deployment pipeline, unclear story acceptance criteria, and inconsistent code formatting. Limited capacity means only one can be addressed next sprint. How should the team decide which to tackle first?
options: Prioritize the issue with the highest impact on product quality and team effectiveness — likely unclear acceptance criteria, as it causes the most defect injection and rework
Address the fastest improvement first (code formatting) to demonstrate quick progress and build team morale
Let the Scrum Master decide based on their assessment of technical feasibility and team capability
Address all three at reduced effort levels simultaneously so none becomes a blocking issue
explanation: Retrospective improvement actions should be prioritized by quality and effectiveness impact. Unclear acceptance criteria cause upstream defect injection — the most expensive problem type. Addressing root causes of rework (quality defects) delivers more value than cosmetic improvements like code formatting, aligning with PMI's prevention-first mindset.
explanation_vi: Các hành động cải tiến retrospective nên được ưu tiên theo tác động đến chất lượng và hiệu quả. Tiêu chí chấp nhận không rõ ràng gây ra lỗi ngược dòng — loại vấn đề tốn kém nhất. Giải quyết nguyên nhân gốc rễ của việc làm lại (lỗi chất lượng) mang lại giá trị nhiều hơn các cải tiến mỹ quan như định dạng code, phù hợp với tư duy ưu tiên phòng ngừa của PMI.

question: A PM is performing quality control on a completed deliverable. Which of the following are quality control activities? (Select 3)
options: Measuring product dimensions against the specification tolerance
Auditing the team's adherence to the approved testing process
Running automated tests to verify software functionality against requirements
Conducting a peer review of the process documentation template
Reviewing test results to identify defects before customer delivery
explanation: QC activities inspect actual outputs: measuring against specs (A), running automated tests on the product (C), and reviewing test results to find defects (E) are all output-inspection activities. Auditing process adherence (B) is QA. Reviewing a process template (D) is also QA/planning, not QC output inspection.
explanation_vi: Các hoạt động QC kiểm tra đầu ra thực tế: đo theo thông số kỹ thuật (A), chạy kiểm thử tự động trên sản phẩm (C) và xem xét kết quả kiểm thử để tìm lỗi (E) đều là hoạt động kiểm tra đầu ra. Kiểm toán tuân thủ quy trình (B) là QA. Xem xét mẫu tài liệu quy trình (D) cũng là QA/lập kế hoạch, không phải kiểm tra đầu ra QC.
